scenes.title: What is Constructor Gear?

cards.label: Toolkit libraries for reusable platform building blocks — OData, canonical errors, secure DB ORM, API middleware, security, and more
cards.labelHtml: Toolkit libraries for reusable platform building blocks — OData, canonical errors, secure DB ORM, API middleware, security, and more

cards.label: System gears for platform and control-plane capabilities — events, API gateway, auth, tenant resolution, registries, and outbound integration
cards.labelHtml: System gears for platform and control-plane capabilities — events, API gateway, auth, tenant resolution, registries, and outbound integration

cards.label: Domain gears for business capabilities — GenAI, OSS, and BSS services with ready-to-use public APIs
cards.labelHtml: Domain gears for business capabilities — GenAI, OSS, and BSS services with ready-to-use public APIs

scenes.intro:
A Gear is the unit of composition in Constructor Gears.
Constructor Gears is a secure, modular XaaS development framework and middleware that helps you build XaaS products by composing gears:
Every layer has built-in Defense-in-depth - multi-tenancy, granular access control
scenes.introHtml:
A Gear is the unit of composition in Constructor Gears.
Constructor Gears is a secure, modular XaaS development framework and middleware that helps you build XaaS products by composing gears:
Every layer has built-in Defense-in-depth - multi-tenancy, granular access control
scenes.outro: It is not a ready-to-use service — it is a set of well-integrated libraries that XaaS vendors compose into their own services and products.
scenes.outroHtml: It is not a ready-to-use service — it is a set of well-integrated libraries that XaaS vendors compose into their own services and products.

scenes.title: For whom?

cards.label: XaaS / SaaS vendors building products on a governed, multi-tenant backbone
cards.labelHtml: XaaS / SaaS vendors building products on a governed, multi-tenant backbone

cards.label: Platform & product teams who want security and tenancy for free
cards.labelHtml: Platform & product teams who want security and tenancy for free

cards.label: GenAI builders needing chat, RAG, model management, agents, tools
cards.labelHtml: GenAI builders needing chat, RAG, model management, agents, tools

cards.label: On-prem / edge vendors shipping single-binary appliances
cards.labelHtml: On-prem / edge vendors shipping single-binary appliances

cards.label: Enterprises embedding Gears into an existing platform via plugins
cards.labelHtml: Enterprises embedding Gears into an existing platform via plugins

scenes.intro:
Vendors who use Gears decide which gears to include, how to combine them,
and where to run them — from edge devices to Kubernetes clusters.
scenes.introHtml:
Vendors who use Gears decide which gears to include, how to combine them,
and where to run them — from edge devices to Kubernetes clusters.

scenes.title: Non-goals

cards.label: Optimize for minimalism / lowest barrier to entry
cards.labelHtml: Optimize for minimalism / lowest barrier to entry
lines: Prioritizes explicit structure, security, governance, and evolvability.

cards.label: Ship a rich catalog of end-user services
cards.labelHtml: Ship a rich catalog of end-user services
lines: It is the foundation on which vendors build complete SaaS offerings.
linesHtml: It is the foundation on which vendors build complete SaaS offerings.

cards.label: Replace cloud infrastructure or PaaS
cards.labelHtml: Replace cloud infrastructure or PaaS
lines: Gears are libraries and building blocks, not a ready PaaS

scenes.intro: Constructor Gears deliberately does not:
scenes.introHtml: Constructor Gears deliberately does not:

cards.label: Secure by default (defense-in-depth) — security is structural, not opt-in, validated at build time.
cards.labelHtml: Secure by default (defense-in-depth) — security is structural, not opt-in, validated at build time.

cards.label: Architecture enforced at compile time - use custom lint rules via dylint
cards.labelHtml: Architecture enforced at compile time - use custom lint rules via dylint

cards.label: Three-tier gears hierarchy — toolkit, System gears, Service gears.
cards.labelHtml: Three-tier gears hierarchy — toolkit, System gears, Service gears.

cards.label: Composable libraries, vendor-controlled deployment — own API + DB, SDK facades local vs. remote.
cards.labelHtml: Composable libraries, vendor-controlled deployment — own API + DB, SDK facades local vs. remote.

cards.label: Local-first shift-left development - run and test everything locally, LLM-friendly
cards.labelHtml: Local-first shift-left development - run and test everything locally, LLM-friendly

cards.label: Pre-integrated XaaS backbone — tenancy, licensing, quota, usage, events — all replaceable via plugins.
cards.labelHtml: Pre-integrated XaaS backbone — tenancy, licensing, quota, usage, events — all replaceable via plugins.

cards.label: Extensible domain model and API data types via the Global Type System (GTS).
cards.labelHtml: Extensible domain model and API data types via the Global Type System (GTS).

scenes.title: Principle 1 — Secure by default (defense-in-depth)

cards.label: Static checks — lints + CI catch violations at build time
cards.labelHtml: Static checks — lints + CI catch violations at build time

cards.label: Authentication — gateway validates tokens → injects SecurityContext
cards.labelHtml: Authentication — gateway validates tokens → injects SecurityContext

cards.label: Authorization — PolicyEnforcer → PDP → decision + constraints → AccessScope
cards.labelHtml: Authorization — PolicyEnforcer → PDP → decision + constraints → AccessScope

cards.label: DB scoping — SecureConn applies AccessScope as automatic WHERE clauses
cards.labelHtml: DB scoping — SecureConn applies AccessScope as automatic WHERE clauses

cards.label: Credentials — credstore; Outbound — oagw egress policy
cards.labelHtml: Credentials — credstore; Outbound — oagw egress policy

scenes.intro:
The architecture makes the insecure path harder than the secure one.
The platform owns a linear security data-path:
scenes.introHtml:
The architecture makes the insecure path harder than the secure one.
The platform owns a linear security data-path:
scenes.outro: There is no "unscoped" shortcut to accidentally use.
scenes.outroHtml: There is no "unscoped" shortcut to accidentally use.

scenes.title: Principle 2 — Architecture enforced at compile time

cards.label: tools/dylint_lints/ — a dedicated Dylint suite that checks:
cards.labelHtml: tools/dylint_lints/ — a dedicated Dylint suite that checks:
lines: contract-layer purity, DTO placement & schema derives
versioned REST paths, mandatory OperationBuilder metadata
OData extension usage, GTS* identifier correctness
linesHtml: contract-layer purity, DTO placement & schema derives
OData extension usage, GTS* identifier correctness

cards.label: Runs alongside Clippy + CI → violations fail fast, before review or runtime
cards.labelHtml: Runs alongside Clippy + CI → violations fail fast, before review or runtime

scenes.intro: Custom static analysis is a core architectural mechanism, not a coding aid.
scenes.introHtml: Custom static analysis is a core architectural mechanism, not a coding aid.
scenes.outro: "Shift-left": architecture that lives in markdown decays; Dylint makes it executable. GTS* = Global Type System identifers like gts.cf.core.events.v1~a.b.c.d.v1~
scenes.outroHtml: "Shift-left": architecture that lives in markdown decays; Dylint makes it executable. GTS* = Global Type System identifers like gts.cf.core.events.v1~a.b.c.d.v1~

scenes.title: Dylint — compile-time architecture validation

cards.label: Domain-layer isolation — no infra imports (sqlx, sea_orm, ...) in domain/
cards.labelHtml: Domain-layer isolation — no infra imports (sqlx, sea_orm, ...) in domain/

cards.label: Direct-SQL restriction — raw SQL only in migration infrastructure
cards.labelHtml: Direct-SQL restriction — raw SQL only in migration infrastructure

cards.label: Versioned REST paths — endpoints must be /<gear>/v1/...
cards.labelHtml: Versioned REST paths — endpoints must be /<gear>/v1/...

cards.label: Contract-layer purity — SDK/contract crates stay transport-agnostic
cards.labelHtml: Contract-layer purity — SDK/contract crates stay transport-agnostic

cards.label: Mandatory OperationBuilder metadata — auth posture, error responses, schemas declared
cards.labelHtml: Mandatory OperationBuilder metadata — auth posture, error responses, schemas declared

cards.label: OData extension usage — standardized $filter / $orderby / $select helpers
cards.labelHtml: OData extension usage — standardized $filter / $orderby / $select helpers

cards.label: GTS identifier correctness — valid IDs; no schema_for! on GTS structs
cards.labelHtml: GTS identifier correctness — valid IDs; no schema_for! on GTS structs

scenes.intro: Repository-specific lints in tools/dylint_lints/ make the design executable — not just documented - code won't compile in case of violation:
scenes.introHtml: Repository-specific lints in tools/dylint_lints/ make the design executable — not just documented - code won't compile in case of violation:
scenes.outro: Runs Dylint in CI → architecture violations fail the build before review or runtime.
scenes.outroHtml: Runs Dylint in CI → architecture violations fail the build before review or runtime.

scenes.title: Principle 3 — Three-tier gears hierarchy

cells: Low-level substrate: API middleware, DB access, errors, transport, security, observability, macros
Control plane: API gateway, authn/authz, tenancy, events, resource groups, type registry
Business/domain: serverless runtime, GenAI subsystems, chat, file parser

scenes.caption: All tiers follow the same Toolkit patterns, SDK conventions, and security model.

scenes.title: Principle 4 — Composable libraries, vendor-controlled deployment

cards.label: Constructor Gears ships libraries, not services
cards.labelHtml: Constructor Gears ships libraries, not services

cards.label: Each gear is infrastructure-, DB- and deployment-agnostic
cards.labelHtml: Each gear is infrastructure-, DB- and deployment-agnostic

cards.label: Gears talk via a Rust-native SDK that facades local vs. remote calls
cards.labelHtml: Gears talk via a Rust-native SDK that facades local vs. remote calls

cards.label: DB-agnostic persistence (SeaORM SecureConn) + infra-agnostic cluster primitives
cards.labelHtml: DB-agnostic persistence (SeaORM SecureConn) + infra-agnostic cluster primitives

scenes.outro: Refactor internals freely — as long as the SDK contract stays compatible.
scenes.outroHtml: Refactor internals freely — as long as the SDK contract stays compatible.

scenes.title: Principle 5 — Local-first shift-left development

cards.label: Run gears locally to exercise real business logic — no Jenkins, Ansible, K8s
AI-assisted tooling can inspect the diff and suggest which tests should run
Coverage is easy to collect, and diff coverage makes it clear whether tests actually touched changed code
Fast feedback keeps regressions visible before CI and before any deployment

scenes.intro:
Composable libraries make it possible to run the full business logic locally on a
developer machine.
scenes.introHtml:
Composable libraries make it possible to run the full business logic locally on a
developer machine.
scenes.outro: If it runs locally, it fails locally — long before a pipeline or release stage.

cards.label: Multi-tenancy · permissions & roles· licensing & quota · usage collection
cards.labelHtml: Multi-tenancy · permissions & roles· licensing & quota · usage collection

cards.labelHtml: Swap Constructor Gears' authn-resolver, tenant-resolver, etc. for vendor systems

cards.labelHtml: Integrate an existing product catalog / provisioning / license engine via plugins

scenes.title: Principle 7 — Extensible domain model via GTS

cards.label: Most gears expose an extensible domain model
cards.labelHtml: Most gears expose an extensible domain model

cards.label: The Global Type System provides versioned, schema-validated type definitions
Add new event types, settings, model attributes, permissions, license types — without modifying existing gears
cards.labelHtml: Add new event types, settings, model attributes, permissions, license types — without modifying existing gears

cards.label: Open-closed via plugins: host defines a plugin interface in its SDK; plugins register as scoped ClientHub clients keyed by GTS instance ID
cards.labelHtml: Open-closed via plugins: host defines a plugin interface in its SDK; plugins register as scoped ClientHub clients keyed by GTS instance ID

cards.labelHtml: CRUD handlers customizable via hooks/callbacks as serverless functions/workflows

cards.label: Spec-Driven Development — PRD → Design & ADR → Feature before code
cards.labelHtml: Spec-Driven Development — PRD → Design & ADR → Feature before code

cards.labelHtml: Code validation — custom Dylints + Clippy + tests + fuzzing + audits in CI

cards.labelHtml: Quality First — 90%+ coverage target across unit / integration / E2E / perf / security

scenes.title: Why Rust?

cards.label: Compile-time safety — eliminates broad classes of memory & concurrency bugs
cards.labelHtml: Compile-time safety — eliminates broad classes of memory & concurrency bugs

cards.label: Great fit for reusable platform code — predictable perf, explicit interfaces
cards.labelHtml: Great fit for reusable platform code — predictable perf, explicit interfaces

cards.label: Static analysis as architecture — Clippy + custom Dylints enforce rules at build
cards.labelHtml: Static analysis as architecture — Clippy + custom Dylints enforce rules at build

cards.label: Operational efficiency — low footprint → realistic local/edge runs & E2E
cards.labelHtml: Operational efficiency — low footprint → realistic local/edge runs & E2E

cards.label: AI-friendly - great compiler and linters assistant to catch problems early
cards.labelHtml: AI-friendly - great compiler and linters assistant to catch problems early

cards.label: Universal language — cloud/on-prem/edge, kernel + even frontend, mobile
cards.labelHtml: Universal language — cloud/on-prem/edge, kernel + even frontend, mobile

Building a platform layer for long-lived XaaS systems — concurrency,
correctness, and maintainability matter more than raw implementation speed.

cards.label: Atomic contract evolution — core contracts + all consumers updated together
cards.labelHtml: Atomic contract evolution — core contracts + all consumers updated together

cards.labelHtml: Shared quality gates — one set of lints, CI, tests, security scanning

cards.labelHtml: Integrated architecture validation — reqs, design, code and tests in one place

cards.label: AI-friendly development — single context improves the feedback loop
cards.labelHtml: AI-friendly development — single context improves the feedback loop

cards.label: Full traceability and index - suitable for autonomous & LLM-assisted development
cards.labelHtml: Full traceability and index - suitable for autonomous & LLM-assisted development

scenes.intro: Constructor Gears is a co-evolving platform, not a loose collection of packages.
scenes.introHtml: Constructor Gears is a co-evolving platform, not a loose collection of packages.

scenes.title: Deployment shapes (FUTURE)

cards.label: Single-node — all gears in one process → edge, on-prem appliances, dev/test
cards.labelHtml: Single-node — all gears in one process → edge, on-prem appliances, dev/test

cards.label: Multi-node — gears across processes/machines over REST/gRPC, no orchestration → bare-metal on-prem
cards.labelHtml: Multi-node — gears across processes/machines over REST/gRPC, no orchestration → bare-metal on-prem

cards.label: Kubernetes — containerized services, full orchestration, cloud-native ops
cards.labelHtml: Kubernetes — containerized services, full orchestration, cloud-native ops

scenes.intro: Same gear code & API — three physical shapes via runtime.type: local | oop:
scenes.introHtml: Same gear code & API — three physical shapes via runtime.type: local | oop:
scenes.outro: Develop locally single-node → deploy bare-metal → scale to K8s, no rewrites.

scenes.title: Repository structure
scenes.code:
cyberware-rust/
├─ libs/            # Toolkit — runtime substrate (middleware, DB, errors, security, macros)
├─ gears/
│  ├─ system/       # Control plane (api-gateway, authn/authz, tenant, registries, oagw)
│  └─ <service>/    # Business/domain & GenAI gears (mini-chat, file-parser, ...)
├─ apps/            # Executable apps composing gears (example server)
├─ examples/        # Reference gears (users-info, oop-gears, fips-probe)
├─ tools/           # Dylints, CI scripts, fuzz targets
└─ docs/            # Manifest, gears registry, toolkit_unified_system, arch, security

scenes.title: Part 4 — What is a Gear in a nutshell?
Gear = independent capability domain

cards.label: Owns its public API via an SDK crate (<name>-sdk) — traits, models, errors
cards.labelHtml: Owns its public API via an SDK crate (<name>-sdk) — traits, models, errors

cards.label: Owns its data behind SecureConn + AccessScope (no raw DB access)
cards.labelHtml: Owns its data behind SecureConn + AccessScope (no raw DB access)

cards.label: Is discovered at link time via inventory, initialized in dependency order
cards.labelHtml: Is discovered at link time via inventory, initialized in dependency order

cards.label: Composes through the typed ClientHub (in-proc) or gRPC SDKs (remote)
cards.labelHtml: Composes through the typed ClientHub (in-proc) or gRPC SDKs (remote)

cards.label: Is extensible via built-in or external plugins
cards.labelHtml: Is extensible via built-in or external plugins

cards.label: Published at crates.io independently with it's own version
cards.labelHtml: Published at crates.io independently with it's own version

scenes.intro: A Gear:
scenes.outro: A Gear is a self-contained, composable unit of business capability that plugs into the platform. Directory is still gears/<name>/.
scenes.outroHtml: A Gear is a self-contained, composable unit of business capability that plugs into the platform. Directory is still gears/<name>/.

scenes.title: Gear anatomy — DDD-light layers

cells: DTOs, Axum handlers, OperationBuilder routes + OpenAPI. PEP entry point
Business logic, SDK trait impl, #[domain_model] types, PEP enforcement

scenes.caption: SDK-first: implementation depends on the SDK, never the reverse #[domain_model] macro rejects infra types (sqlx, axum, reqwest...) in domain

scenes.title: Gear execution model

cards.label: In-process (default) — gears share one runtime; communicate via typed ClientHub clients; wired by Tookit
cards.labelHtml: In-process (default) — gears share one runtime; communicate via typed ClientHub clients; wired by Tookit

cards.label: Out-of-process — gears as separate processes over gRPC
cards.labelHtml: Out-of-process — gears as separate processes over gRPC

scenes.intro: The logical model stays identical regardless of the physical boundary.
scenes.introHtml: The logical model stays identical regardless of the physical boundary.
scenes.outro: Switch modes with a YAML field (runtime.type) — no code changes.
scenes.outroHtml: Switch modes with a YAML field (runtime.type) — no code changes.

scenes.title: Gear composition — compiled-in vs selectable

cards.label: Core system gears are linked into the host binary and discovered via inventory
cards.labelHtml: Core system gears are linked into the host binary and discovered via inventory

cards.label: Optional gears/plugins are included by Cargo feature flags such as mini-chat, static-authn, static-authz, static-credstore
cards.labelHtml: Optional gears/plugins are included by Cargo feature flags such as mini-chat, static-authn, static-authz, static-credstore

cards.label: Feature flags decide what code is present; runtime config decides which vendor, plugin instance, or capability is used
cards.labelHtml: Feature flags decide what code is present; runtime config decides which vendor, plugin instance, or capability is used

cards.label: Built-in plugins run in-process and register scoped ClientHub clients
cards.labelHtml: Built-in plugins run in-process and register scoped ClientHub clients

cards.label: External plugins/gears keep the same SDK contract but can run out-of-process over gRPC
cards.labelHtml: External plugins/gears keep the same SDK contract but can run out-of-process over gRPC

scenes.intro: Constructor Gears favors explicit, reproducible composition over opaque runtime magic.
scenes.introHtml: Constructor Gears favors explicit, reproducible composition over opaque runtime magic.
scenes.outro: Result: small vendor-specific binaries, deterministic SBOMs, and the same extension model locally or remotely.

scenes.title: Gear lifecycle (platform-owned)
scenes.code:
pre_init → DB migration → init → post_init(barrier)
        → REST wiring → gRPC wiring → start → ... → stop
scenes.caption: HostRuntime runs a shared, ordered phase sequence: System gears run first where required post_init begins only after all init hooks complete Shutdown runs in reverse dependency order with a graceful-stop deadline Cancellation tokens propagate so background work cooperates with shutdown

scenes.title: Plugins & extensibility

cards.label: Gateway Gear defines a plugin interface in its SDK + registers the plugin schema in the Types Registry
cards.labelHtml: Gateway Gear defines a plugin interface in its SDK + registers the plugin schema in the Types Registry

cards.label: Plugin Gears implement the interface, register as scoped ClientHub clients keyed by GTS instance ID
cards.labelHtml: Plugin Gears implement the interface, register as scoped ClientHub clients keyed by GTS instance ID

cards.label: Host discovers plugins at runtime, routes to the selected one via a vendor config field
cards.labelHtml: Host discovers plugins at runtime, routes to the selected one via a vendor config field

cards.label: Built-in plugin — compiled in, own business-logic crate + optional dedicated DB
cards.labelHtml: Built-in plugin — compiled in, own business-logic crate + optional dedicated DB

cards.label: External plugin — separate deployable, own API handler + business logic + DB
cards.labelHtml: External plugin — separate deployable, own API handler + business logic + DB

scenes.outro: Add a new backend (e.g. a custom auth provider) = write a plugin. Host stays unchanged.

cards.labelHtml: Makes route metadata, schemas, and contracts part of the architecture, not afterthoughts

scenes.intro: Gears Toolkit is the central framework that turns the gear architecture into a reusable runtime discipline.
scenes.introHtml: Gears Toolkit is the central framework that turns the gear architecture into a reusable runtime discipline.
scenes.outro: One predictable operational model for every gear across every deployment shape.

scenes.intro:
OperationBuilder is the authoritative route-registration mechanism — one place declares:
Result:
scenes.introHtml:
OperationBuilder is the authoritative route-registration mechanism — one place declares:
Result:

scenes.title: API handler registration example
scenes.code:
pub fn register_routes(
    mut router: Router,
    openapi: &dyn OpenApiRegistry,
    service: Arc<ConcreteService>,
) -> Router {
    router = OperationBuilder::get("/simple-user-settings/v1/settings")
        .operation_id("simple_user_settings.get_settings")
        .summary("Get user settings")
        .authenticated()
        .require_license_features::<License>([])
        .handler(handlers::get_settings)
        .json_response_with_schema::<dto::SimpleUserSettingsDto>(
            openapi,
            StatusCode::OK,
            "Settings retrieved",
        )
        .error_401(openapi)
        .error_500(openapi)
        .register(router, openapi);

    router.layer(Extension(service))
}

scenes.title: Request lifecycle
scenes.source:
%% Reconstructed from docs/img/request_sequence.png for the Slidey overview deck. sequenceDiagram autonumber actor Client participant Gateway as API Gateway
(middleware stack) participant AuthN as AuthN Resolver
(+ vendor plugin → IdP) participant Handler as Gear Handler
(api/rest, PEP entry) participant Domain as Domain Service participant PEP as PolicyEnforcer (PEP) participant PDP as AuthZ Resolver (PDP)
(+ vendor plugin) participant Repo as Repository + SecureConn participant DB as Gear DB Client->>Gateway: HTTP request + Bearer token Note over Gateway: SetRequestId / Trace / Timeout /
BodyLimit / CORS / MIME / RateLimit rect rgb(54, 66, 92) Note over Gateway,AuthN: Authentication (AuthN middleware) Gateway->>Gateway: resolve route auth requirement alt route is .authenticated() Gateway->>AuthN: authenticate(bearer_token) AuthN->>AuthN: vendor plugin validates / introspects token (IdP) AuthN-->>Gateway: AuthenticationResult { SecurityContext } Gateway->>Gateway: inject Extension SecurityContext else missing token Gateway-->>Client: 401 Unauthorized (MISSING_BEARER) end Gateway->>Gateway: ScopeEnforcement (token scopes) + License check end Gateway->>Handler: dispatch to Axum handler + SecurityContext Handler->>Handler: deserialize DTO / extract Path and OData query Handler->>Domain: service.method(&ctx, args) rect rgb(45, 78, 58) Note over Domain,PDP: Authorization (PEP → PDP) Domain->>PEP: access_scope(ctx, ResourceType, action, resource_id) PEP->>PDP: EvaluationRequest(subject, action, resource, context, token_scopes) PDP->>PDP: vendor plugin evaluates policies
(+ tenant/RG hierarchy) PDP-->>PEP: decision + constraints alt decision = false or constraints missing PEP-->>Domain: EnforcerError::Denied → 403 Forbidden (fail-closed) else allowed PEP->>PEP: compile constraints → AccessScope PEP-->>Domain: AccessScope end Domain->>Repo: repo.op(&conn, &scope, ...) Repo->>Repo: secure().scope_with(&scope)
SQL WHERE (tenant + constraints) Repo->>DB: scoped query / insert / update DB-->>Repo: rows (0 rows on point op → 404) Repo-->>Domain: domain model(s) end Domain-->>Handler: domain result or DomainError Handler-->>Gateway: ApiResult T -> JSON / RFC-9457 Problem Gateway->>Gateway: ErrorMapping (canonical errors), Trace span close Gateway-->>Client: HTTP response

scenes.title: Request lifecycle — who owns what

cards.label: API Gateway owns authentication (token → SecurityContext) and fast license validation
cards.labelHtml: API Gateway owns authentication (token → SecurityContext) and fast license validation

cards.label: Gear domain services own licensing and authorization — call PolicyEnforcer (PEP)
cards.labelHtml: Gear domain services own licensing and authorization — call PolicyEnforcer (PEP)

cards.label: AuthZ Resolver (PDP) returns a decision + row-level constraints
cards.labelHtml: AuthZ Resolver (PDP) returns a decision + row-level constraints

cards.label: Constraints compile to an AccessScope → SecureConn applies SQL WHERE
cards.labelHtml: Constraints compile to an AccessScope → SecureConn applies SQL WHERE

cards.label: Gear code never parses tokens or resolves tenancy directly
cards.labelHtml: Gear code never parses tokens or resolves tenancy directly

scenes.intro:
Gateway middleware order:
SetRequestId → Trace → Timeout → BodyLimit → CORS → MIME → RateLimit → ErrorMapping → Auth → ScopeEnforcement → License → Router
scenes.introHtml:
Gateway middleware order:
SetRequestId → Trace → Timeout → BodyLimit → CORS → MIME → RateLimit → ErrorMapping → Auth → ScopeEnforcement → License → Router

scenes.title: Security — PDP/PEP authorization

cards.label: PDP (AuthZ Resolver plugin) — evaluates policies (RBAC/ABAC/ReBAC, vendor's choice)
cards.labelHtml: PDP (AuthZ Resolver plugin) — evaluates policies (RBAC/ABAC/ReBAC, vendor's choice)

cards.label: PEP (domain gear) — compiles constraints into SQL WHERE clauses
cards.labelHtml: PEP (domain gear) — compiles constraints into SQL WHERE clauses

cards.label: Returns predicates, not resource IDs → O(1) authz overhead, correct pagination, accurate counts
cards.labelHtml: Returns predicates, not resource IDs → O(1) authz overhead, correct pagination, accurate counts

cards.label: Fail-closed: denied / unreachable PDP / missing constraints → 403
cards.labelHtml: Fail-closed: denied / unreachable PDP / missing constraints → 403

scenes.intro:
PDP — the Policy Decision Point that evaluates authorization policies.
PEP — the Policy Enforcement Point that applies the decision in the gear.
NIST SP 800-162 PDP/PEP model, built on OpenID AuthZEN + query-level constraints.
scenes.introHtml:
PDP — the Policy Decision Point that evaluates authorization policies.
PEP — the Policy Enforcement Point that applies the decision in the gear.
NIST SP 800-162 PDP/PEP model, built on OpenID AuthZEN + query-level constraints.

cards.label: Entities derive Scopable with #[secure(tenant_col, resource_col, owner_col, type_col)]
cards.labelHtml: Entities derive Scopable with #[secure(tenant_col, resource_col, owner_col, type_col)]

cards.label: AccessScope carries four dimensions: tenant, resource, owner, type
cards.labelHtml: AccessScope carries four dimensions: tenant, resource, owner, type

cards.label: SecureConn auto-maps PEP properties → columns → WHERE clauses
cards.labelHtml: SecureConn auto-maps PEP properties → columns → WHERE clauses

cards.label: Tenant topology: single-root hierarchy; every resource has owner_tenant_id
cards.labelHtml: Tenant topology: single-root hierarchy; every resource has owner_tenant_id

cards.label: Barriers (e.g. self_managed) restrict parent visibility into subtrees
cards.labelHtml: Barriers (e.g. self_managed) restrict parent visibility into subtrees

cards.label: Resource groups add optional access-control grouping within tenants
cards.labelHtml: Resource groups add optional access-control grouping within tenants

cards.label: Rust safety baseline — memory safety, strict lints
cards.labelHtml: Rust safety baseline — memory safety, strict lints

cards.label: Split AuthN / AuthZ as separate system services
cards.labelHtml: Split AuthN / AuthZ as separate system services

cards.label: SecurityContext propagation — explicit data, no thread-local magic
cards.labelHtml: SecurityContext propagation — explicit data, no thread-local magic

cards.label: Outbound boundary — oagw centralizes egress policy + credential injection
cards.labelHtml: Outbound boundary — oagw centralizes egress policy + credential injection

cards.label: Credential handling — credstore + secrecy-aware types
cards.labelHtml: Credential handling — credstore + secrecy-aware types

cards.label: Static & CI gates — Clippy, Dylints, cargo-deny, CodeQL, fuzzing, Scorecard/Snyk/Aikido
cards.labelHtml: Static & CI gates — Clippy, Dylints, cargo-deny, CodeQL, fuzzing, Scorecard/Snyk/Aikido

scenes.title: Security — FIPS 140-3
scenes.caption: Build with --features fips to route TLS crypto through a FIPS 140-3 validated module: One rustls 0.23 state machine, pluggable CryptoProvider per OS Offers only FIPS-approved suites (AES-GCM, P-256/384, SHA-256/384) Fails closed on Windows when OS FIPS mode is off

scenes.title: Global Type System (GTS)

cards.label: Globally unique, human-readable, versioned identifiers for types & instances
cards.labelHtml: Globally unique, human-readable, versioned identifiers for types & instances

cards.label: gts.cf.core.events.event.v1~ style IDs as a platform contract surface
cards.labelHtml: gts.cf.core.events.event.v1~ style IDs as a platform contract surface

cards.label: GTS JSON Schemas generated directly from Rust types → registered in Types Registry
cards.labelHtml: GTS JSON Schemas generated directly from Rust types → registered in Types Registry

cards.label: The non-HTTP counterpart to OpenAPI: describes plugin specs, events, permissions
cards.labelHtml: The non-HTTP counterpart to OpenAPI: describes plugin specs, events, permissions

cards.labelHtml: GTS-specific Dylints validate identifier correctness

A powerful extension-point architecture — virtually every API can be extended
without modifying core code.

scenes.title: Testing architecture

cards.label: Unit & integration — in-process, SQLite :memory:, mocked AuthZ
cards.labelHtml: Unit & integration — in-process, SQLite :memory:, mocked AuthZ
lines: domain invariants, validation, error chains, DTO conversions, seeding — no HTTP, no real DB
linesHtml: domain invariants, validation, error chains, DTO conversions, seeding — no HTTP, no real DB

cards.label: End-to-end — pytest vs. a running cyberware-server with a real DB
cards.labelHtml: End-to-end — pytest vs. a running cyberware-server with a real DB
lines: JSON wire format, real AuthZ wiring, DB-specific SQL, cross-gear SDK boundaries

scenes.intro:
A dual-layer strategy with an explicit zero-overlap rule:
Unit-test gate: (1) deterministic domain logic? (2) atomic & fast? (3) removing it lowers confidence?
scenes.introHtml:
A dual-layer strategy with an explicit zero-overlap rule:
Unit-test gate: (1) deterministic domain logic? (2) atomic & fast? (3) removing it lowers confidence?

scenes.title: How to build & run
scenes.code:
git clone --recurse-submodules <repository-url>
cd cyberware-rust

make build      # build libs + example server
make example    # run the example server
# → API docs at http://127.0.0.1:8087/cw/docs

# Health checks
curl http://127.0.0.1:8087/cw/health   # detailed JSON
curl http://127.0.0.1:8087/healthz      # liveness "ok"
scenes.caption: Run modes: SQLite (config/quickstart.yaml), no-DB ( config/no-db.yaml), or --mock.

scenes.title: Configuration
scenes.caption: Env overrides with CYBERFABRIC_ prefix, e.g. CYBERFABRIC_DATABASE_URL=postgres://...

scenes.title: How to run tests
scenes.code:
make check           # full quality gate: fmt + clippy + test + security
make test            # unit tests (workspace)
make test-sqlite     # integration tests (SQLite, no external DB)
make e2e-local       # E2E — builds + starts server automatically
make coverage-unit   # unit-test coverage
make fuzz            # fuzz smoke tests (30s/target)
scenes.caption: On Windows (no make): python tools/scripts/ci.py check Full strategy & CI details: docs/TESTING.md.

scenes.title: Why Constructor Gears is a good choice for your project

cards.label: XaaS-friendly - built-in multi-tenancy, licensing, usage, etc
cards.labelHtml: XaaS-friendly - built-in multi-tenancy, licensing, usage, etc

cards.label: Security by architecture — AuthN/AuthZ, tenancy, scoped DB access, FIPS 140-3
cards.labelHtml: Security by architecture — AuthN/AuthZ, tenancy, scoped DB access, FIPS 140-3

cards.label: Compile-time governance — linters and Dylints detect violations before runtime
cards.labelHtml: Compile-time governance — linters and Dylints detect violations before runtime

cards.label: Composable gear model — single code - multiple builds and deployments
cards.labelHtml: Composable gear model — single code - multiple builds and deployments

cards.label: Shift-left productivity — all-in-one gears process for local build and test
cards.labelHtml: Shift-left productivity — all-in-one gears process for local build and test

cards.label: Extensible by design — custom API data types, plugins, serverless gears
cards.labelHtml: Extensible by design — custom API data types, plugins, serverless gears

cards.label: Operations friendly — OpenAPI, OData, canonical errors, observability, migrations, and lifecycle are standard
cards.labelHtml: Operations friendly — OpenAPI, OData, canonical errors, observability, migrations, and lifecycle are standard
